behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real git history evaluation plus PR-state lookup. Mocking git/PR adapters can hide real failures in commit ancestry/age computation and no-PR detection wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo with `main` and a feature branch containing older commits not reachable from `main`; run real detection logic (including actual git subprocess calls) with a real or test PR backend state showing no open PR; assert branch is classified stale.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: The risk is in bootstrap wiring (startup path, current-branch discovery, and warning emission). Unit tests of formatter/classifier miss failures in session initialization and git-context acquisition.
behaviors.test_description: Run session bootstrap in a real repo checkout on a stale branch and capture startup output/log stream; verify warning appears once and includes actionable branch context (branch name/age/next action).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure local mapping logic with no I/O boundary; real failures are logic regressions in one function.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test for classifier inputs (clean, stale-no-pr, boundary values) asserting exact label outputs and deterministic repeatability across repeated calls.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly depends on live repo and PR state. Mocked dependencies can pass while real command/API interactions or rendering integration fail.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end in a real repository context with real PR-state source (or full-fidelity test service) for two scenarios: clean branch and stale-no-pr branch; assert rendered output text differs correctly.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: False-positive risk sits at git/PR-state interaction boundaries, especially PR lifecycle semantics (open vs merged). Mock PR objects can hide real-state interpretation mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: In real repo + PR-state integration, test branches linked to (a) open PR and (b) merged PR, with stale-looking commit age; assert detector does not emit `stale-no-pr` for either.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: This failure mode is tied to real git graph transformations (rebase/squash) and ancestry resolution via git behavior. Mock commit graphs frequently miss edge semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use real git operations to simulate rebase and squash-merge scenarios, then run detection against resulting graph/PR state; assert branch is no longer marked `stale-no-pr` when work is effectively integrated.
